source: Curated from hardware-database/02-tmc-drivers/tmc_drivers_reference.md — interface and register/field capabilities verified against the Kalico driver modules (klippy/extras). Electrical figures are datasheet-typical and vary with board/sense-resistor/cooling. Keyed by the Klipper config section name (the model Klipper reports).

drivers.name: TMC2208
drivers.current_note: ~1.2 A RMS typical (≈2 A peak); Klipper code cap 2.0 A
drivers.notes: Silent budget driver. No StallGuard/CoolStep, so no sensorless homing. The TMC2225 is a 2208-class refresh (configure as [tmc2208]).

drivers.name: TMC2209
drivers.current_note: ~1.4–1.7 A RMS typical (≈2.8 A peak); Klipper code cap 2.0 A
drivers.notes: The default modern UART driver. StallGuard4 (sgthrs) → sensorless homing on X/Y. Very common on Voron/Ender boards. The TMC2226 is an improved 2209 (configure as [tmc2209]).

drivers.name: TMC2130
drivers.current_note: ~1.2 A RMS typical (≈2 A peak); Klipper code cap 2.0 A
drivers.notes: Older SPI driver. StallGuard2 (sgt, −64…63) → sensorless homing. Supports SPI daisy-chain.

drivers.name: TMC2240
drivers.current_note: ~2.0–3.0 A RMS (board/Rref/current_range dependent); set via GLOBALSCALER + current_range
drivers.notes: Modern high-current driver with an on-chip temperature/supply-voltage sensor and over-temp/over-voltage prewarning. StallGuard4 (sg4_thrs) and StallGuard2 (sgt). Picks UART or SPI at runtime. Runs hot at high PWM frequency.

drivers.name: TMC2660
drivers.current_note: ~1.8–2.4 A RMS; Klipper code cap 2.4 A
drivers.notes: Older mid-power SPI driver (legacy Duet/RADDS-era). No StealthChop — not silent. StallGuard2 (sgt, SGCSCONF).

drivers.name: TMC5160 / 5161
drivers.current_note: ~3 A RMS internal FETs; board-limited, often 4.5 A+ with externals; Klipper code cap 10.6 A
drivers.notes: High-power / high-voltage driver (~8–60 V) for big motors — large CoreXY, IDEX, gantries. StallGuard2 (sgt). The module also covers the TMC2160; the 5161 is the integrated-FET sibling.
